---
title: "About"
---

I am a Ph.D. Candidate in Sociology in the University of Minnesota. Broadly, I study 
crime, punishment, and quantitative methodology and statistics. My dissertation
investigates the iatrogenic effects of punishment on crime and other aspects 
of social life, using unique causal identification strategies. To read more 
about my research projects [click here](research.html). To download my Curriculum Vitae [click here](cv.html). 

Prior to graduate school, I received my BA in Sociology and Psychology, 
*Summa cum Laude*, from [Concordia College](https://www.concordiacollege.edu/) in 2014, and was the winner of the 
2014 Farden Memorial Prize in Sociology. I also played saxophone in 
[The Concordia Band](https://www.concordiacollege.edu/student-life/music-ensembles/bands/the-concordia-band/) 
and [The Concordia College Jazz Ensemble I](https://www.concordiacollege.edu/student-life/music-ensembles/jazz-ensembles/). 

I also have a dog, meet Pig!

<center>
![](images/pig.jpg)
</center>
